{{Quote|Inactivity has always been difficult for me, despite my aunts' attempt to drill patience into my nature. Back on Alderaan, Tia, Rouge, and Celly would say, "All action is foolish without reflection. Wait and learn, Leia—and learn how to wait."|Leia Organa|<ref name="CTE" />}}
'''Rouge Organa''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who was a member of the [[House of Organa/Legends|Organa family]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] and the sister of [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail]], [[Celly Organa/Legends|Celly]] and [[Tia Organa]], as well as the adopted aunt of [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. A strict dowager, Rouge helped her brother raise the young Leia by inculcating her with the codes and protocol of high court life. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Rouge Organa [[Death/Legends|died]] in the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction of Alderaan]].

==Biography==
===Early years===
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], Rouge Organa was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in the last decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]],<ref name="CotJ" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of pristine beauty that had been and a center of culture and education for many a [[Standard century/Legends|century]].<ref name="EGPM">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref> From birth, she was destined for a glamorous life, as the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] of the most [[Noble/Legends|noble]] and wealthy [[House of Organa/Legends|House Organa]] coursed through her veins. She was the daughter of the [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Organa (viceroy)|Organa]] and one of four siblings, the others being [[Tia Organa|Tia]], [[Celly Organa/Legends|Celly]] and [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor]].<ref name="CotJ" /> When she finished school and [[Age of responsibility|came of age]], Rouge was brought to a debutante ball on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city of the Republic. By custom, young noblewomen attended levees in hopes they could find a suitable husband of high lineage.<ref name="COTJnote">In [[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']], the presentation of young noblewomen to the Court was said to be "customary among the old Houses." Furthermore, Leia Organa's aunts were appalled when she refused to participate until she could do so as a Senator in her own right, which indicates they had complied with the custom themselves.</ref> Eventually, Rouge Organa got [[Marriage/Legends|married]] and started a family of her own.<ref name="TFUG">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref> In the meantime, her brother Bail Prestor Organa went on to become the Viceroy and [[First Chairman/Legends|First Chairman]] of the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]] and the husband of [[King/Legends|Queen]] [[Breha Organa/Legends|Breha Antilles]].<ref name="Databank">{{DB|character|bailorgana|Organa, Bail}}</ref>

===A princess' tutor===
Following the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a three-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide conflict, Rouge's brother adopted the late [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala's]] daughter, a baby called [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]].<ref name="Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith">{{Film|III}}</ref> Rouge Organa and her two sisters, Tia and Celly, helped their brother raise his adopted child, moving to the [[Royal Palace of Alderaan/Legends|Royal Palace]] of [[Aldera/Legends|Aldera]].<ref name="TPLD" /> By the time Leia was an infant, Rouge Organa had become a widow.<ref name="CotJ" />

Rouge and her sisters hired deportment teachers to tutor young Leia, wishing to make a "proper" princess out of their tombyish niece,<ref name="CotJ" /> including the redoubtable [[Vesta|Madame Vesta]], [[Headmaster/Legends|headmistress]] of [[Alderaan Select Academy for Young Ladies]]. The Organa sisters would often tend to the Princess' dress and [[Hair/Legends|hair]] themselves, much to Leia's annoyance.<ref name="TPLD" /> In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Rouge accompanied Leia Organa, now a young adult and a Senator, to a levee on Coruscant along with her siblings. When her niece noticed the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] jeweled concubines and asked her about them, Rouge assumed a superior air and answered that a [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] was not obligated to speak with their sort.<ref name="CotJ" />

===Dead with her planet===
{{Quote|Millions of lives vaporized. My father, my best friend Winter, my aunts, my teachers, my friends. All gone.|Leia Organa, on those who perished in the destruction of Alderaan|<ref name="CTE" />}}
[[File:DestructionOfAlderaan-ANH.png|thumb|right|Rouge Organa died along with her homeworld.]]
[[0 BBY/Legends|One year later]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] decided to subject all Alderaanians to a radical punishment, following their Viceroy's involvement with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. The Imperial [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Tarkin]] decided to use his new [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], to sow the seeds of terror and keep the seditious star systems in line. The peaceful planet Alderaan vanished with a single green blast from the Death Star's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]], with the captive Princess Leia watching in horror.<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> Although her aunts had made her girlhood tough and sometimes unpleasant, Leia Organa was immediately struck with grief when she realized they were gone forever.<ref name="CTE" /> Many years later, the Princess, now the [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] of a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], would often remember her Aunt Rouge.<ref name="CotJ" />

==Personality==
{{Quote|Only spaceport types go in for the Semi-Dries, my dear. You really must cultivate a more refined taste.|Rouge Organa|<ref name="CotJ" />}}
Rouge Organa was a fair-skinned Human female who sported long hair that she kept bound,<ref name="TPLD" /> as was customary among Alderaanian adults,<ref name="TCS">''[[The Crystal Star]]''</ref> and would adorn them with [[Plant/Legends|flowers]].<ref name="TPLD" /> In the evening, before dinner, she enjoyed having servants tending to her hair in front of a gilt-framed mirror in [[Rouge Organa's boudoir|her boudoir]].<ref name="CotJ" /> A refined woman born into wealth, Rouge Organa enjoyed wearing iridescent [[Old Republic bronze]] earrings, a taste she shared with her sisters. By contrast, she despised semi-dry [[Wine/Legends|wines]], considering them unrefined and only good enough for [[Spacer/Legends|space-hoppers]].<ref name="CotJ" /> A gossipy<ref name="Egc">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> and proud noblewoman, she overtly despised Palpatine's Imperial concubines.<ref name="CotJ" /> Rouge Organa valued patience, and she tried to inculcate this virtue into her niece.<ref name="CTE" /> The dowager attached great importance to etiquette, and she would not hesitate to lecture other people of high-rank about their offspring's public behavior.<ref name="CotJ" /> She also had a very strict idea of the proper dress code for a self-respecting woman.<ref name="CotJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Organas.png|thumb|left|150px|Bail Organa and his sisters with Princess Leia]]
Princess Leia's three dowager aunts were first mentioned in [[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']], is a [[1995]] novel written by [[Barbara Hambly]]. More specifically, Rouge Organa was mentioned by name eleven times throughout the novel, which is twice as much as Celly and more than thrice as much as Tia.<ref name="CotJ" /> Although Rouge, Tia and Celly Organa were created in the absence of a mother figure for the young Leia, early sources such as [[Star Wars (1977) 53|''Star Wars'' (1977) 53]] suggest that a woman whom Leia called "mother" survived until the destruction of Alderaan.<ref name="LGFA">[[Star Wars (1977) 53|''Star Wars'' (1977) 53]]</ref>

In [[1998]], Rouge Organa received a passing mention in [[Jude Watson]]'s ''[[Star Wars Journal: Captive to Evil]]'', a retelling of the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] from Princess Leia's point of view.<ref name="CTE" />

In "[[The Princess Leia Diaries]]," a short comic written by [[Jason Hall]] that appeared in [[Star Wars Tales 11|''Star Wars Tales'' 11]], Bail Organa's three sisters were pictured for the first time. However, Rouge Organa has not been clearly identified with any of the three aunts from the comic.<ref name="TPLD" />

Since the late Queen [[Mazicia Organa]] was stated to be Bail Organa's mother in ''[[Star Wars Blueprints: Rebel Edition]]'',<ref name="SWBRE">''[[Star Wars Blueprints: Rebel Edition]]''</ref> it is possible that she was also the mother of his sisters. However, that particular issue has not been addressed yet.
